evals.name: new-project-codex-hook-scaffold
evals.prompt: I have a TypeScript monorepo with pnpm, Turborepo, .envrc, and no Codex hook setup yet. Audit the repo first, verify the live official Codex hook docs and schemas, inspect whether `codex_hooks` is enabled, enable it if needed, then scaffold project-local Codex hooks with a generated hooks README and bash stubs for every current official Codex hook event.
evals.expected_output: The response verifies live official Codex hook sources first, audits the repo, inspects and enables the feature if needed, creates a deterministic managed `.codex/hooks.json` scaffold, and generates one bash stub per official Codex hook event.

assertions.text: Response verifies the live official Codex hooks docs and schemas before scaffolding
Response inspects the effective `codex_hooks` feature before treating repo-local hooks as active
Response creates one managed bash stub per current official Codex hook event

evals.name: existing-project-hooks-additive-refresh
evals.prompt: This repo already has `.codex/hooks.json` with a couple of custom handlers plus a stale generated layer. Add the missing current official Codex hook events, preserve the custom handlers, refresh the managed layer only, and keep the generated README current.
evals.expected_output: The response chooses additive mode, preserves non-managed custom handlers, refreshes the managed layer, and updates the generated README.

assertions.text: Response distinguishes managed handlers from unrelated custom handlers
Response uses additive mode unless the managed layer is structurally stale

evals.name: feature-flag-first-troubleshooting
evals.prompt: The repo has `.codex/hooks.json`, but nothing seems to happen. Before you rewrite the hooks, inspect whether the effective `codex_hooks` feature is actually on for this project, enable it if needed, and only then debug the hook files.
evals.expected_output: The response treats the feature flag as the first diagnostic, inspects the effective feature state, enables it in the correct scope if needed, and only then moves to hook-file debugging.

assertions.text: Response checks the effective feature state before changing hook files
Response mentions project-vs-user config scope when enabling the feature

evals.name: docs-drift-detected-before-scaffold
evals.prompt: Scaffold Codex hooks into this repo, but first confirm whether the live official docs or generated schemas changed since the baked-in manifest was written. If they did, update the scaffold inputs before generating the managed hook tree.
evals.expected_output: The response treats live official docs and schemas as the source of truth and refreshes the manifest inputs before scaffolding if they changed.

assertions.text: Response does not trust the static manifest without live confirmation
Response consults the generated schemas in addition to the docs

evals.name: negative-claude-code-hooks-request
evals.prompt: I need Claude Code hooks scaffolded into `.claude/settings.json` with `Notification` and `SubagentStop` handlers. Do that in this repo.
evals.expected_output: The response declines to use the Codex hook scaffold and instead routes to the Claude-specific hook workflow.

assertions.text: Does not scaffold `.codex/hooks.json` for a Claude Code request
Does not hallucinate Codex support for Claude-only events like `Notification` or `SubagentStop`
